sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 13g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for meats, fat quality is one of the main tie-breakers after protein.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 13g. omega 3 is doing serious work here. for meats, fat quality is one of the main tie-breakers after protein.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 0g. carbs do not matter much here. meats usually live or die on protein quality and what comes with the fat.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 20.4g. the protein quality is strong. for meats, strong protein is expected, so the rest decides how high it climbs.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin b12 at 117% DV, vitamin d at 66% DV stand out most. that gives the food real vitamin support, not just a tiny bonus.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin b12 at 117% DV, vitamin d at 66% DV stand out most. that gives the food real vitamin support, not just a tiny bonus.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 4% DV, zinc at 4% DV stand out most. the mineral support is still pretty limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: biggest pros. widely recognised omega-3 powerhouse because that gives it a real category edge. very satisfying as a protein source because that makes it practical in real meals. brings premium nutrient reputation because that fits its strong health reputation.

sections.narration: biggest cons. can be expensive because price can make it less practical. quality varies with sourcing because sourcing changes the real-world quality. needs good storage and handling because it is less convenient than shelf-stable options.
